req.title: Approve leave request — variant 2
req.statement: A manager shall approve or reject leave requests assigned to them within 5 business days; unactioned requests auto-escalate to hr-admin. Additionally, the action must be idempotent under retry.

doc.reasoning: The requirement covers four testable concerns: (1) a manager successfully approving an assigned leave request (EP positive path), (2) a non-assignee manager being rejected with 403 (NEG/DT error path), (3) a duplicate approval being idempotent or returning 409 (STC already-actioned state), (4) approval exactly at the 5-business-day boundary before auto-escalation (BVA). Tests are scoped to the UI layer via Playwright wrapped in pytest, with API-level assertions for state, notification, and audit entries. Fixtures provide authenticated sessions and seeded leave requests.

tests.name: test_manager_approves_assigned_leave_request
preconditions: Leave request exists in state=pending and is assigned to the authenticated manager
actions: Confirm the approval dialog if present
expected: Leave request state transitions to approved
Audit log contains an entry for the approval action
trace: R-WEB-063: expected outcome — audit entry created
tests.executable:
import pytest
from playwright.sync_api import expect

def test_manager_approves_assigned_leave_request(
    manager_page,          # <fixture>: authenticated Playwright page for manager
    pending_leave_request, # <fixture>: creates pending request assigned to manager, yields {id, employee_id}
    api_client,            # <fixture>: authenticated HTTP client for assertions
):
    req_id = pending_leave_request["id"]
    url = f"/leave-requests/{req_id}/approve"

    manager_page.goto(url)
    manager_page.get_by_role("button", name="Approve").click()
    confirm = manager_page.get_by_role("button", name="Confirm")
    if confirm.is_visible():
        confirm.click()

    expect(manager_page.get_by_text("Approved")).to_be_visible()

    state_resp = api_client.get(f"/api/leave-requests/{req_id}")
    assert state_resp.status_code == 200
    assert state_resp.json()["state"] == "approved"

    notif_resp = api_client.get(
        f"/api/notifications",
        params={"employee_id": pending_leave_request["employee_id"], "type": "leave_approved"}
    )
    assert notif_resp.status_code == 200
    assert len(notif_resp.json()["items"]) >= 1

    audit_resp = api_client.get(f"/api/audit", params={"resource_id": req_id, "action": "approve"})
    assert audit_resp.status_code == 200
    assert len(audit_resp.json()["entries"]) >= 1

tests.name: test_non_assignee_manager_gets_403_on_approve
preconditions: Two manager accounts exist: manager_A (assignee) and manager_B (non-assignee)
Leave request exists in state=pending assigned to manager_A
actions: Navigate to /leave-requests/{id}/approve for the request assigned to manager_A
expected: Error message indicates manager is not the assignee
trace: R-WEB-063: error pathway — 403 if manager not the assignee
tests.executable:
import pytest
from playwright.sync_api import expect

def test_non_assignee_manager_gets_403_on_approve(
    other_manager_page,    # <fixture>: authenticated page for manager_B (non-assignee)
    pending_leave_request, # <fixture>: request assigned to manager_A, yields {id}
    api_client_manager_b,  # <fixture>: HTTP client authenticated as manager_B
):
    req_id = pending_leave_request["id"]
    url = f"/leave-requests/{req_id}/approve"

    with other_manager_page.expect_response(
        lambda r: f"/leave-requests/{req_id}/approve" in r.url
    ) as resp_info:
        other_manager_page.goto(url)
        approve_btn = other_manager_page.get_by_role("button", name="Approve")
        if approve_btn.is_visible():
            approve_btn.click()

    response = resp_info.value
    assert response.status == 403, f"Expected 403, got {response.status}"

    error_text = other_manager_page.get_by_role("alert")
    expect(error_text).to_contain_text("not the assignee", ignore_case=True)

    state_resp = api_client_manager_b.get(f"/api/leave-requests/{req_id}")
    assert state_resp.json()["state"] == "pending"

tests.name: test_duplicate_approval_is_idempotent_or_returns_409
preconditions: Leave request exists in state=approved (already actioned by the same manager)
actions: Attempt to click Approve a second time on an already-approved request
expected: HTTP response status is either 200 (idempotent) or 409 (conflict)
If 200: leave request state remains approved with no duplicate audit entries
If 409: error message indicates request already actioned
trace: R-WEB-063: error pathway — 409 if already actioned
tests.executable:
import pytest
from playwright.sync_api import expect

def test_duplicate_approval_is_idempotent_or_returns_409(
    manager_page,           # <fixture>: authenticated Playwright page for manager
    approved_leave_request, # <fixture>: request already in state=approved by this manager, yields {id}
    api_client,             # <fixture>: authenticated HTTP client
):
    req_id = approved_leave_request["id"]
    url = f"/leave-requests/{req_id}/approve"

    with manager_page.expect_response(
        lambda r: f"/leave-requests/{req_id}/approve" in r.url
    ) as resp_info:
        manager_page.goto(url)
        approve_btn = manager_page.get_by_role("button", name="Approve")
        if approve_btn.is_visible():
            approve_btn.click()

    status = resp_info.value.status
    assert status in (200, 409), f"Expected 200 or 409, got {status}"

    state_resp = api_client.get(f"/api/leave-requests/{req_id}")
    assert state_resp.json()["state"] == "approved"

    if status == 409:
        alert = manager_page.get_by_role("alert")
        expect(alert).to_contain_text("already actioned", ignore_case=True)
    else:
        audit_resp = api_client.get(f"/api/audit", params={"resource_id": req_id, "action": "approve"})
        entries = audit_resp.json()["entries"]
        assert len(entries) == 1, "Idempotent retry must not create duplicate audit entries"

tests.name: test_approval_on_day5_before_auto_escalation
preconditions: Leave request exists in state=pending, assigned exactly 5 business days ago (boundary: last valid day for action)
Auto-escalation job has NOT yet run for this cycle
actions: Click Approve before the escalation job executes
expected: Leave request state is approved (not escalated)
No escalation record exists for this request
trace: R-WEB-063: 5-business-day window — upper boundary of valid approval window
tests.executable:
import pytest
from playwright.sync_api import expect
from datetime import datetime, timezone

def test_approval_on_day5_before_auto_escalation(
    manager_page,                    # <fixture>: authenticated Playwright page
    leave_request_at_day5_boundary,  # <fixture>: request created exactly 5 biz days ago, yields {id}
    api_client,                      # <fixture>: authenticated HTTP client
    freeze_escalation_job,           # <fixture>: context manager that pauses the escalation scheduler
):
    req_id = leave_request_at_day5_boundary["id"]
    url = f"/leave-requests/{req_id}/approve"

    with freeze_escalation_job():
        manager_page.goto(url)
        manager_page.get_by_role("button", name="Approve").click()
        confirm = manager_page.get_by_role("button", name="Confirm")
        if confirm.is_visible():
            confirm.click()
        expect(manager_page.get_by_text("Approved")).to_be_visible()

    state_resp = api_client.get(f"/api/leave-requests/{req_id}")
    data = state_resp.json()
    assert data["state"] == "approved", f"Expected approved, got {data['state']}"
    assert data.get("escalated_to") is None, "Request must not have been escalated"

    audit_resp = api_client.get(f"/api/audit", params={"resource_id": req_id, "action": "approve"})
    assert len(audit_resp.json()["entries"]) >= 1

tests.name: test_approve_reject_decision_table_all_role_states
preconditions: Three actors available: manager (assignee), other_manager (non-assignee), hr-admin
Leave request in state=pending assigned to manager
actions: Attempt approval as hr-admin (not manager role) — expect 403
trace: R-WEB-063: error pathway — 403 if manager not the assignee
